sanskrit:
यः शास्त्रविधिमुत्सृज्य वर्तते कामकारतः।
न स सिद्धिमवाप्नोति न सुखं न परां गतिम्॥
transliteration:
yaḥ śāstra-vidhim utsṛjya vartate kāma-kārataḥ
na sa siddhim avāpnoti na sukhaṁ na parāṁ gatim
literal_meaning: Whoever, abandoning scriptural ordinance, acts under the impulse of desire, attains neither success, nor happiness, nor the supreme goal.
contextual_meaning: Acting on unchecked impulse severs the internal compass that guides a person toward lasting fulfillment; without an external-into-internalized ethical framework, the mind loops in short-term gratification and long-term regret.
psychological_state: A person caught in the dopamine-chasing cycle who intellectually knows better yet feels unable to stop, producing chronic low-grade dysphoria and a sense of life 'going nowhere'.
trigger_scenario: Scrolling social media until 2 a.m., promising 'just one more,' then waking exhausted, skipping the gym, and binge-eating while rationalizing 'I deserve it.'
anchor_text: Let conscience, not craving, steer your choices.
